text.title: growth-conversion-optimization Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for growth-conversion-optimization as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Stopping tests early when results look good at 80% confidence.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: At 80% confidence the false-positive rate is ~30%, not 5%. "We have a winner" declared on day 4 pollutes the learning library with phantom gains.

failure-mode.description: Using heatmaps as the only research source before forming hypotheses.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Heatmaps reveal where users click but not why they leave. Pair with session recordings and exit surveys to triangulate intent; heatmap-only hypotheses have a lower win rate.

failure-mode.description: Copying "best practice" changes from industry case studies without local research.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Urgency timers and social-proof-above-fold tactics fail to replicate without local data confirming the same friction exists in your specific audience.

failure-mode.description: Running a major redesign as an A/B test.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Multivariate changes cannot isolate which element caused any measured effect; use user research and prototype testing instead.

failure-mode.description: No learning library entry after a test concludes — win or loss.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without documentation, teams re-run the same failed tests and lose accumulated institutional knowledge. The library is the primary long-term asset of a CRO program.
